---
title: "Task 1: Filter, select, and mutate"
number-sections: false
---

[← Back to session page](../08_dplyr.qmd)

:::{.callout-note}

## Find the solution here after the session ends.

```{=html}
<script>const course_day = 2;</script>
<script src="../../timer_solution.js"></script>
<p class="r_class_solution" style="display:none">
  <a href="../solutions/dplyr-solution-1.html" target="_blank">Click here for the solution.</a>
</p>
```
:::

## Getting started

A helpful resource for this task is the [dplyr cheatsheet](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rstudio/cheatsheets/master/data-transformation.pdf).

Before you start, make sure to load the `tidyverse` package.

```{r}
#| eval: false
library(tidyverse)
```

We will continue working with the `penguins` data set.

## Filter penguins

**Find all penguins that ...**

1. ... have a bill length between 40 and 45 mm.

2. ... are of the species Adelie or Gentoo.

3. ... lived on the island Dream in the year 2007.

## Remove missing values

4. Remove all penguins with missing values for `sex` using `drop_na()`.

## Select columns

5. Select only the variables `species`, `sex`, and `year`.

6. Select only columns that start with `"bill"`.

## Add new columns

7. Add a column with the ratio of bill length to bill depth.

8. Add a column with abbreviations for the species (Adelie = A, Gentoo = G, Chinstrap = C).

## Combine with the pipe

9. Use the pipe to combine multiple steps: start with `penguins`, remove rows with missing `sex`, keep only Adelie penguins, and select only `species`, `sex`, and `body_mass`.

## For the fast ones

- Use `filter_out()` to exclude penguins from Torgersen island, then select only `species`, `island`, and `flipper_len`.

- Create a `size_category` column with `case_when` based on body mass (small < 3500, medium < 5000, large >= 5000). 
  - **Extra**: Do it in a pipe that also removes NAs and selects only `species`, `body_mass`, and `size_category`.
